task: Respond to the clarified goal: users run a desktop agent with Datalox MCP/pack, Datalox records structured debugging trajectory data, and the company sells validated datasets to AI companies.
step: align product goal around dataset business
summary: User clarified that the repo goal is not primarily direct user skill reuse; the intended business is a desktop agent plus Datalox MCP instrumentation that records structured debugging trajectories and sells validated datasets/eval assets to AI companies. Response should reframe trace->note->skill as user-facing improvement/acquisition and schema export as the commercial product.
observations: Desktop agent is the distribution and task-capture surface.
Datalox MCP is better framed as the instrumentation, consent, verification, and export layer.
Trace->note->skill still matters because it improves the user's agent and creates higher-quality future trajectories, but the repo's commercial boundary should optimize for verified dataset rows.
Selling to consumers is hard; the stronger wedge is give users a useful desktop agent and monetize aggregated, consented, redacted, verified trajectory datasets for companies.
title: User clarified that the repo goal is not primarily direct user skill reuse; the intended business is a desktop agent pl…
signal: User corrected the product goal toward B2B data sales from desktop-agent usage rather than direct consumer sale.
interpretation: The corrected framing makes sense: direct user value creates supply, while structured verified trajectory export creates the sellable asset.
recommendedAction: Update product language and schema around a two-sided loop: user agent utility first, consented trajectory dataset export second; keep Datalox MCP as the named data/knowledge protocol.
